_comment: Copy this file to watchtower.config.json and edit for your environment. The real config is gitignored in the public watchtower repo; in the runtime repo it stays tracked so the scheduled scan can read it.
_promptsRoot_help: Optional. If set, the scan reads security-scan-prompt.md and claude-md-template.md from this directory instead of <watchtowerRoot>/prompts/. Useful when you want to point your runtime at a separately-cloned copy of the public watchtower repo so you only maintain one methodology file. Omit this key (or set null) to use the default (prompts live inside the runtime repo).
_selfReschedule_help: Optional, default true. When true, Phase 0 self-arms the next run as a one-shot timestamp (now + scanCadenceDays) using whatever scheduler fired this run. Set to false ONLY if a recurring EXTERNAL scheduler owns the cadence (a Windows Scheduled Task, a cron entry, a systemd timer) — Phase 0 then stands down instead of arming, and won't emit the 'arming failed' warning. The skill never inspects or verifies the external job; that's your environment, not the skill's job.
_emailDebrief_help: Optional. If present AND `RESEND_API_KEY` is set in ~/.claude/.env, Phase E emails a 5-section debrief (what changed / what stands out / what needs addressing / what's good / what's bad) after every scheduled scan. Requires a Resend account (https://resend.com) with a verified sending domain. To disable: omit this block or set it to null. To test without sending: `echo '# test' | python scans/send_debrief.py --subject test --dry-run`.
_categories_help: Categories are the chips that show up on the dashboard filter bar. Keys are the display names; projects reference them by exact-match in their `category` field below AND in window.APPS[*].category. Each value can have: color (string accent OR {bg, border, accent, tag} object), excludeFromStats (drop from aggregate stats), sortOrder (lower = earlier in filter bar), dim (faded card styling, like an archive bin). Categories not listed here still render — they just get a neutral gray chip. Delete this map to fall back to fully auto-derived neutral chips.

projects._comment: folder may be a nested subpath under portfolioRoot — e.g. a client app grouped under a parent folder. The scan joins portfolioRoot + folder, so forward slashes work on every OS. slug must still be unique and filename-safe.
